calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-25-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Dennis Vann, Esq. appeared in person for Durrell Milton (Petitioner/Administrator). Jeffrey Coleman, Esq. appeared via Zoom for Probate Advance LLC.

The matter is called. Mr. Coleman stated that he was appearing to object. The Court stated that the matter would be continued and that the objections need to be reduced to writing. Mr. Vann expressed concerns with the delay noting that his client had taken an unprecedented move to make this happen, that his client withdrew funds from his 401k to move this along, and that his client only has 60 days to return the funds or the withdrawn funds become taxable income. Mr. Coleman provided additional argument noting that there was a concern that the asset could be lost. Mr. Vann stated that proof was provided to the Court, that his client transferred funds from his personal account at Patelco to an estate account at Patelco, and that there are enough funds to cover the advances and funds to the beneficiaries. Mr. Vann also stated that status on receipts should be set and that they are prepared to write the checks. The Court stated that Probate Advance LLC is owed $197,000.00, that it finds Mr. Vann's statement that his client made efforts to move this along convincing, and inquired if Mr. Vann has proof that the funds were deposited into the estate account. Mr. Vann confirmed that the funds are in the estate account and stated that proof is in the supplemental declaration filed on 1/23/22. The Court passed this matter until 3:00 p.m. for counsel to review the filings.

At the second call of the matter, Mr. Coleman is present via Zoom and Mr. Vann is present in court. Mr. Coleman stated that he had reviewed the filing and had no objection noting that they would welcome an order entered for a payout.

At the conclusion of comments, the Court granted the petition for final distribution (order signed). The clerk will file the order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Cho. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Continued To Oct-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dennis Vann, via Zoom, for Milton Durrell, Personal Representative; Konstantine Demiris, via Zoom, for Probate Advance, LLC.

Mr. Vann reports that his client is attempting to obtain a loan to buy-out the other beneficiaries' interest in the real property and if that is not possible, the property will likely need to be sold. Mr. Vann requests a continuance of sixty (60) days accordingly. The court advises that it is inclined to continue this matter for the amount of time requested with the caveat that either a petition for final distribution or a petition to approve status of Administration needs to be filed before the next hearing date. Mr. Demiris asks the court to order an accounting and offers advocacy for his request. The court acknowledges Mr. Demiris' position and advises that it will not be ordering an accounting at this time.

The matter is continued to Oct-25-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.
